medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Mr. ___ is a ___ year old male with recurrent metastatic NSCLC, CAD s/p NSTEMI and VT storm s/p ICD who presents with progressive dyspnea. Recently admitted with syncope and unstable VT due to R on T phenomenon. Treated with defibrillation, transferred for ICD placement and started on metoprolol and verapamil. Noticed cough and dyspnea 2 weeks ago, diagnosed with PNA and treated with amoxicillin and doxycycline. Symptoms progressed to fatigue, dizziness, presyncope upon standing, and dyspnea on exertion, leading to presentation at the ED.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Metastatic Lung Cancer, L adrenal Metastasis, CAD s/p NSTEMI, HTN, HLD, OSA, GERD.
medical_history.family_history: Father with COPD, Identical twin with HCM.

physical_examination.general_examination: Tired, NAD, BP 132/61, HR 81, R 18, T 98.6, SpO2 97% RA, decreased breath sounds on left side with mild crackles.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: CN II-XII intact. Cardiovascular: Regular heart sounds, ICD over L anterior chest c/d/I without erythema.
